questions.text: Walk me through your first 30 days as an ops manager joining a company with a 12-point drop in on-time delivery.
Tell me about a time you identified an operational bottleneck and resolved it. What metrics moved as a result?
How would you handle a major supply chain disruption that hits with no warning?
Walk me through your triage process when three urgent incidents land in your queue at the same time.
How do you set the operating metrics that your team is measured against, and how often do you change them?
How would you approach reducing operating cost by 15 percent without harming customer experience?
Walk me through how you apply root cause analysis. Use the 5 Whys or fishbone in real time.
A critical KPI is trending the wrong direction for three weeks. Walk me through how you would investigate.
Demand spikes 40 percent overnight. How do you manage the operational response?
Describe an SOP you wrote and rolled out. How did you make sure people actually followed it?
Walk me through how you would set up a daily standup for a 30-person operations team.
How do you handle an underperformer on a critical shift?
Tell me about a time you resolved a conflict between two departments.
Tell me about a process improvement you led. What was the before-after impact?
Describe a time you had to deliver bad operational news to a customer.
Tell me about a time you cut costs without sacrificing quality.
Describe a time you had to lead change through a team that resisted it.
Explain inventory turnover and OTIF. What are healthy benchmarks in your industry?
Walk me through your approach to a Six Sigma DMAIC project on a real problem.
What tools do you use to monitor operational health day to day, and what would you reach for first if a number looks wrong?
